problem_id: Build a complete Snake game with replay system allowing users to play live games and watch ghost replays simultaneously on the same grid at variable playback speeds

goals.description: Users can play a complete Snake game with proper mechanics (movement, collision, food, scoring)
success_criteria: Snake responds to arrow key input and moves in commanded direction
Snake grows by 1 segment when consuming food
Game ends on wall collision or self-collision
Score increments by 1 for each food item consumed
Speed increases as snake length increases

goals.description: Users can record and replay completed games at variable playback speeds
success_criteria: Each game state is recorded throughout play session
Replays can be selected from a list of completed games
Replay plays back at 0.5×, 1×, 2×, or 4× speed
Replay produces bit-exact sequence matching original game
User can stop and restart a replay

goals.description: Users can play live games while viewing a ghost replay simultaneously
success_criteria: Ghost snake appears on same grid as live snake
Ghost snake is visually distinct (different color/style) from live snake
Ghost snake does not interfere with live game outcome
Live game and ghost replay run independently without collision logic between them
User can start new live game while ghost replay is running

functional_requirements.description: Snake moves in response to arrow key input
acceptance_criteria: Arrow Up moves snake up (negative Y)
Arrow Down moves snake down (positive Y)
Arrow Left moves snake left (negative X)
Arrow Right moves snake right (positive X)
Direction change happens on next game tick, not immediately
Rapid key presses are buffered (does not cause reverse direction in single move)

functional_requirements.description: Collision detection for walls and snake body
acceptance_criteria: Game ends when snake head touches grid boundary
Game ends when snake head touches any segment of its own body
Collision is checked after each tick before rendering
Game-over state prevents further input and movement
Food appears at random empty grid cell at game start
When snake head touches food, food is consumed and removed
New food spawns at a different random empty cell after consumption
Food never spawns on snake body or outside grid
Score starts at 0
Score increments by 1 when food is consumed
Score is displayed on UI during active game
Score is shown in replay list next to completed games
Initial game speed is 5 FPS (200ms per tick)
Speed increases by 1 FPS for each food segment consumed (PR: snake length = 3 + food_count)
Speed is capped at reasonable maximum (e.g., 15 FPS) to prevent unplayability
Speed change is reflected immediately in game tick rate

functional_requirements.description: State serialization capturing all game states
acceptance_criteria: Every game state (snake position, food location, score, tick count) is recorded
State recorded at each tick during active play
Serialization format enables accurate replay without loss of information
All recorded states are stored in memory or persistent storage (localStorage or IndexedDB)
Completed game states can be retrieved for replay selection
UI displays list of completed games with score and timestamp
User can select a replay from the list
Selected replay plays back all recorded states in sequence
Playback follows speed multiplier selected by user (0.5×, 1×, 2×, 4×)
User can pause and resume replay
User can stop replay to return to main menu
Ghost snake is rendered from replay state data
Ghost snake is visually distinct from live snake (e.g., lighter color, different opacity, dashed line)
Ghost snake is rendered on same grid layer as live snake
Ghost and live snakes can occupy same grid cells simultaneously
Rendering does not cause performance degradation

functional_requirements.description: Simultaneous live game and ghost replay
acceptance_criteria: User can start new live game while ghost replay is running
Live game and ghost replay tick independently
Live game collision logic applies only to live snake, not ghost
Ghost snake state does not affect food spawn or live snake movement
Both game states render correctly on same canvas/grid
User can stop either game independently
Replayed game produces identical sequence of states as original
All snake positions match frame-by-frame
All food positions match frame-by-frame
Score at each tick matches original
No variation in replay across multiple playbacks of same game

functional_requirements.description: Prevent reverse direction in single move
acceptance_criteria: Pressing right then left in rapid succession does not cause snake to move into itself
Input is buffered to next tick; direction change only applies on next movement
Only the most recent input is processed if multiple keys pressed between ticks
Snake cannot move into its own body as a result of rapid key presses

functional_requirements.description: Game UI controls (new game, pause, resume, stop)
acceptance_criteria: New Game button starts a fresh game with snake at center, random food, score 0
Pause button stops game tick and input processing
Resume button continues paused game
Stop button ends current game and returns to main menu
Replay list button shows completed games available for replay
Identical sequence of states produced across multiple replays
No floating-point errors or rounding affecting grid positions
Deterministic random number generation for food spawning during replay
No variation in timing or state due to browser performance variance
Canvas rendering completes in <16.67ms per frame (60 FPS target)
Live game tick calculation completes in <50ms
Simultaneous live + ghost replay does not drop below 30 FPS
No visible lag or stuttering during active play or replay
Single game recording (100+ ticks) does not exceed 100KB in memory
Multiple replays can be stored simultaneously without excessive memory usage
Serialization format is optimized (e.g., delta encoding or compact binary)
localStorage or IndexedDB can store 10+ completed games
Ghost snake is easily distinguishable from live snake in all grid states
UI controls respond to clicks within 100ms
Replay speed changes take effect within 1 tick
No visual artifacts or rendering glitches when ghost and live snakes overlap
Game runs in Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+
Canvas 2D API supported in all target browsers
localStorage or IndexedDB available for persistence
No browser-specific quirks or polyfills required

constraints.rationale: Requirements state application must be client-side only for GitHub Pages deployment
Web-based game requires JavaScript/Canvas for rendering and state management
Larger grids would exceed target FPS; typical Snake implementations use 20-30 cell width

constraints.description: Replay speed limited to 0.5×, 1×, 2×, 4× discrete multipliers
constraints.rationale: Continuous speed not requested; discrete speeds simplify UI and implementation

constraints.description: No external game libraries or frameworks
constraints.rationale: Game engine should be implemented from first principles for learning value

out_of_scope: AI autopilot (A* or BFS pathfinding) — listed as optional stretch goal
Time rewind (undo last N moves) — listed as optional stretch goal
Leaderboard with shareable links — listed as optional stretch goal
Obstacles and power-ups — listed as optional stretch goal
Mobile touch controls — not specified in requirements
Multiplayer or online play — not mentioned in requirements
Difficulty levels or game modes — not specified

unit_tests: GridState: cell initialization, snake body segments, food positions
CollisionDetector: wall collision, self-collision, food consumption
SnakeMovement: direction changes, movement in all 4 directions, buffered input handling
GameSpeed: FPS calculation based on snake length, speed capping
StateSerializer: serialization and deserialization of game state
FoodSpawner: random spawn on empty cells, no spawn on snake body
ReplayEngine: playback at different speeds, state accuracy
integration_tests: Complete game flow: start → move → eat → grow → speed increase → collision → end
Input buffering: rapid key presses do not cause reverse direction
State recording: all ticks recorded accurately during play
Replay + live game: simultaneous play without interference
browser_tests: E2E: Launch game → move snake → eat food → verify score and speed → end game
E2E: Complete game → select from replay list → play at different speeds → verify bit-exactness
E2E: Start live game → start ghost replay → verify coexistence → move live snake → stop game
